codeReview.summary: The Governor and GovernanceToken contracts themselves look structurally sound, but the feature does not fully satisfy its contract. The new governed deployment rewires MarketVersionRegistry to the timelock/governor path while the updated live risk smoke script still drives those calls as a direct risk-admin AccessManager action, and the promised GovernanceToken mint coverage never landed in the lifecycle tests.

issues.description: This script is incompatible with the governed deployment introduced by the same feature. `deployGovernedSystem()` retargets `MarketVersionRegistry.registerVersion` and `activateVersion` to `ROLE_IDS.GOVERNANCE` and grants that role to the timelock (`/home/kpa/polkadot/dualvm/lib/deployment/deployGovernedSystem.ts:96-117`), but `liveRiskAdminSmoke.ts` still executes those registry calls through `managedRegisterVersion` / `managedActivateVersion` as `baseActors.riskAdmin` and even reports `governanceMode: "access-manager-role"` (`liveRiskAdminSmoke.ts:61-91`). After a governed deployment from `scripts/deployGoverned.ts`, the risk admin no longer has authority for these operations, so `npm run risk-smoke:testnet` will fail against the current governed manifest path instead of exercising the new governor stack correctly.

The feature contract explicitly required `test/GovernorLifecycle.ts` to cover GovernanceToken mint + delegate behavior, but this suite never calls `governanceToken.mint(...)` at all. The token-focused cases at lines 64-104 only check initial supply, timestamp clock mode, delegation, and undelegated holders. That means the new AccessManager-gated mint path is entirely unverified and the tests would not catch a broken authority wiring, an inaccessible mint function, or a regression in the intended mintable-via-AccessManager behavior.

`deployGovernedSystem()` returns `...base` without overwriting `base.governance.admin`, even though lines 114-117 transfer AccessManager admin to the timelock and revoke the deployer. `scripts/deployGoverned.ts` works around this by rebuilding `manifest.governance.admin` manually, but any other caller that inspects the helper return value directly will still see the deployer as the governance admin, which is stale metadata for the new governed path.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Solidity worker skill's mandatory 'Write tests FIRST (red)' step does not match how new-contract governance features are actually being implemented. In this run the worker created `GovernanceToken.sol` before any red test existed, then noted in handoff feedback that compiling contracts before writing tests was more practical for this kind of feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/kpa/polkadot/.factory/skills/solidity-worker/SKILL.md:28` requires 'Write tests FIRST (red)'. The transcript skeleton at `/home/kpa/.factory/missions/dc664f26-9086-4d9b-896d-ba8f3bde8d71/worker-transcripts.jsonl:1` shows the worker saying 'I'll begin by creating the Solidity contracts' and creating `GovernanceToken.sol` before `test/GovernorLifecycle.ts`, and the handoff at `/home/kpa/.factory/missions/dc664f26-9086-4d9b-896d-ba8f3bde8d71/handoffs/2026-03-16T21-33-25-892Z__governor-contracts__fa49d260-f1de-4df4-bbd3-d4eebcb2bf3d.json:96-97` explicitly suggests relaxing that rule for new-contract work.

summary: Reviewed the governor-contracts handoff, transcript skeleton, commit diff, and relevant Solidity/TypeScript files. Result: fail, because the governed deployment breaks the updated `liveRiskAdminSmoke.ts` operational path and the required GovernanceToken mint coverage is still missing from `test/GovernorLifecycle.ts`.
